title: triticum condensatum
intro: Triticum condensatum, also known as wild wheat or annual wild wheat, is a type of wild grass that belongs to the wheat family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems. Traditionally, Triticum condensatum is prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be dried and used in herbal supplements or added to soups and stews.
